decks.notes: This deck aims to quickly and efficiently reanimate powerful creatures such as Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. It also utilizes instant speed removal and disruption like Prismatic Ending and Thoughtseize to control the game and protect its combo pieces. By combining graveyard manipulation with powerful creatures and a disruptive gameplan, this deck seeks to win the game through a fast and efficient combo strategy that overwhelms the opponent before they can respond effectively.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble powerful artifact creatures to overwhelm the opponent, using cards like Urza's Saga and Weapons Manufacturing to generate threats. The deck also includes cards like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke for control against opposing strategies, as well as Emry, Lurker of the Loch for recurring key artifacts. The presence of Tormod's Crypt and Claws of Gix indicates a graveyard and sacrifice synergy, while Pithing Needle and Skateboard offer additional flexibility and disruption options. Overall, the deck emphasizes speed, versatility, and aggression through artifact synergies.

This deck focuses on quickly generating mana and play powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Emrakul, the Promised End. It also utilizes cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Blade of the Bloodchief to build a board presence and control the game. The combo aspect comes from using cards like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble to disrupt the opponent while generating advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its powerful creatures and disruptive spells, making it a challenging matchup in the Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures or spells from the graveyard using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. By combining instant speed reanimation with disruptive spells like Teferi, Time Raveler and Force of Negation, the deck can control the game while setting up its combo pieces. A key combo involves using Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Quantum Riddler to generate card advantage and overwhelm the opponent. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and win the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

This Boros Aggro deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a mix of aggressive creatures and burn spells. With cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer leading the charge, the deck can quickly put pressure on opponents. Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge provide additional reach to finish off opponents. The deck is focused on maintaining a fast pace and closing out games before opponents can stabilize, making it a potent force in the Modern format.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Broodscale Bloodchief's ability to grow in power with Blade of the Bloodchief equipped. Emrakul, the Promised End provides a game-ending threat, while Ancient Stirrings and Boseiju, Who Endures help find key pieces. Malevolent Rumble and Glaring Fleshraker disrupt opponents' strategies, while Kozilek's Command provides versatility. Urza's Saga helps fetch combo pieces, with support from Vexing Bauble and Ugin's Labyrinth. The deck also includes ramp and mana acceleration with Eldrazi Temple and Grove of the Burnwillows to cast high-cost spells like Emrakul.

The deck focuses on a combo strategy that doesn't rely on lands to generate mana. It includes a mix of counterspells, card draw, and disruptive spells to protect the combo pieces and control the game. The main win condition is to assemble the Goblin Charbelcher combo and use it to deal massive damage to the opponent. The deck also includes ways to tutor for key pieces and manipulate the opponent's resources. This deck aims to quickly assemble its combo while disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly cycling through cards and casting Living End to bring back powerful creatures from the graveyard, while disrupting the opponent's strategy with counterspells and card manipulation. The key is to efficiently set up the combo and overwhelm the opponent with a board full of creatures. By utilizing cycling cards, cascade effects, and recursion, the deck aims to create a strong board presence and win the game before the opponent can respond effectively.

This deck focuses on quickly and efficiently reanimating powerful creatures from the graveyard using spells like Goryo's Vengeance and Faithful Mending. It also utilizes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect its combo pieces. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces, bringing back creatures like Griselbrand or Atraxa, Grand Unifier to dominate the board and overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes control elements like Solitude and Teferi, Time Raveler to handle threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This deck revolves around utilizing mana ramp cards and land tutors to quickly assemble the pieces needed to cast the powerful Primeval Titan and trigger its ability multiple times. The goal is to amass a large number of lands, including utility lands like Urza's Saga and Boseiju, Who Endures, to overwhelm the opponent with efficient threats like Cultivator Colossus. By using cards like Scapeshift and Green Sun's Zenith, the deck can quickly search for key pieces like Amulet of Vigor and Malevolent Rumble to create explosive combos and close out the game.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to play aggressively and quickly, utilizing low-cost spells and creatures to deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. Cards like Lightning Bolt, Monastery Swiftspear, and Dragon's Rage Channeler help to apply constant pressure, while cards like Expressive Iteration and Preordain help to maintain card advantage and find the right answers. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Boomerang Basics and Academic Dispute to control the opponent's board and disrupt their game plan. The mana base is optimized for consistency and quick access to the mana needed for spells.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays and early pressure, utilizing a mix of creatures, removal spells, and disruption to control the board and outpace the opponent. With a low mana curve and efficient threats, the goal is to quickly overwhelm the opponent and secure a victory before they can stabilize. The deck includes a mix of powerful creatures like Kroxa and Ragavan, along with versatile spells and lands to support the aggressive game plan. By maintaining pressure and adapting to the opponent's strategies, this deck aims to consistently close out games in its favor.

The main focus of this deck is to control the board through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and planeswalker abilities. The deck includes powerful control cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Counterspell, as well as removal options like Fatal Push and Supreme Verdict. The addition of cards like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian and Consult the Star Charts helps the player dig for answers and maintain card advantage. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while slowly building up its own resources to take control of the game and secure a win.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly get aggressive creatures on the battlefield, while also utilizing burn spells and card draw to control the opponent's board and maintain a consistent flow of threats. By efficiently casting low-cost spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart, the deck is able to maintain pressure on the opponent while also benefitting from synergies with cards like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Monastery Swiftspear. Additionally, the deck includes disruptive elements like Slickshot Show-Off and Violent Urge to further disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This UW Control deck focuses on disrupting opponents with counterspells like Counterspell and Force of Negation, while also utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler and Narset, Parter of Veils to control the board and draw cards. The deck also includes removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Solitude to deal with threats, as well as card advantage engines like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive. The goal is to control the game through card advantage, counterspells, and efficient removal spells until the opponent runs out of resources.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and artifacts-matter cards to overwhelm the opponent with quick and aggressive plays. By sacrificing artifacts to cards like Arcbound Ravager and Claws of Gix, as well as utilizing Urza's Saga to create tokens and generate card advantage, the deck aims to outpace the opponent and disrupt their strategy with cards like Tormod's Crypt and Engineered Explosives. With a mix of burn, control, and creature threats, the deck aims to maintain board presence and apply pressure in order to secure a swift victory.
